tx01.text: This is Earth Relay Station 4 to Helix-7. We read your distress beacon. Sending extraction coordinates in fragments — reactor interference is breaking up our signal. Transcribe each transmission exactly as received. One error and the nav computer rejects the sequence.

tx02.text: First coordinate fragment: bearing 227 mark 14, declination offset nine point three. The rescue ship Calloway is already en route. They need the full sequence or they jump blind.

tx03_clear.text: Fragment two confirmed. Secondary coordinate: adjust for solar drift at 0.7 arc seconds per hour. Helix-7, your reactor core is at 61 percent and falling. You have time. Stay calm and keep transcribing.

tx03_static.text: Fragment two — warning, signal degrading — secondary coordinate: adjust for solar drift — corrupted — arc seconds per hour. Helix-7, we are losing you. Reactor reading critical. Do not stop transcribing.

tx04_clear.text: Third fragment locked in. Spatial coordinate complete: final bearing 091 mark 33. Calloway is forty minutes out. Helix-7, you are going to make it home. Two more fragments and the approach is yours.

tx04_warning.text: Third fragment — power surge detected — bearing 091 — station is venting atmosphere on deck two — mark 33. Calloway adjusted course. Eighteen minutes, MORSE. Just keep your hands steady. We are not done yet.

tx04_critical.text: MORSE we are losing telemetry — fragment three — 091 — systems failing — mark 33 — Helix-7 do you copy — the Calloway cannot wait — keep going — there are two fragments left and no time to lose.

tx05_clear.text: Fragments four and five: relative velocity two point one klicks per second, approach corridor delta-seven. Calloway has your spatial fix and is burning to match it. MORSE, the hard part is behind you. One transmission left and you are clear.

tx05_static.text: Fragments four and — interference — relative velocity two point one — corridor delta — seven — we are riding the edge of your signal, MORSE. Reactor wash is climbing. Keep the rhythm. Do not let the static into the numbers. One more.

tx06_clear.text: Final fragment: docking transponder seven-seven-alpha, clamp frequency one-one-four-point-two. That is the whole sequence, MORSE. Read it back clean and the Calloway locks straight onto you. Last transmission. Make it count.

tx06_static.text: Final fragment — transponder seven-seven — alpha — clamp frequency one-one-four — MORSE we are nearly blind on this end. This is the last window the relay can hold open. Send it back. Whatever you have, send it back now.

tx06_critical.text: MORSE — relay is collapsing — transponder seven-seven-alpha — that is all we can give you — no time to repeat it — the Calloway is committing to the pass — type it, type it now, this is the last thing you will hear from us.

end_good.text: Sequence complete. All six fragments locked. Calloway is on final approach, transponder matched, bearing confirmed. MORSE — well done. Open the docking bay and get to the airlock. You brought everyone home.

end_neutral.text: The Calloway found Helix-7 by its heat signature, not its coordinates. A twenty-minute window, not forty. Enough for four of the six crew to make it through the airlock before the reactor hit redline. The debrief would call it a partial success. You would never use that word for it.

end_bad.text: The coordinates the Calloway received were close — close enough that their sensors swept the sector for three full hours before the fuel margin ran out. The log would record: signal corrupted, position unconfirmed. In the silence after the last transmission, you sat at the terminal and listened to static.
